$comment: Resolved mapping for the /pull-new-designs skill (Step 0). Each upstream Claude Design project maps to its committed sync baseline + the app destinations a run reconciles into, so re-runs resolve deterministically instead of re-guessing. 'styling' is this repo's component styling convention; 'namespace' is the design-system bundle namespace used to detect baselines by content, not folder name.

policy.note: Apply an upstream removal (delete the primitive/token locally) when syncing from a COMPLETE design-system import — a kind=design-system project verified complete against its _ds_manifest, where absence reliably means 'removed', so mirror it. From a product/prototype export (a partial slice), NEVER infer removal: absence only means 'not used by these prototypes'. Either way, surface what changed in the report.

snapshot.note: Committed change-key baseline for the /pull-new-designs `sync-plan.mjs` script: per-component DS sourceHashes, token name→value map, and the integrated product lastChangelogVersion. On re-sync, sync-plan diffs a fresh export's index files (bundle header + manifest + _changelog.json) against this snapshot to emit the MINIMAL get_file list (was: a full ~81-file pull). Regenerate AFTER integrating a sync: `node <skill>/scripts/sync-plan.mjs snapshot --from <export>`. The generated runtime (support.js, _ds_bundle.js) is gitignored via design/.upstream/.gitignore — this snapshot replaces the need to commit the ~4200-line bundle body.

app.$comment: Target-repo wiring so the gates/verify are not hardcoded to this repo (the skill reads this). 'src' = roots check-motion scans for *.module.css/keyframes (omit to auto-derive the …/src ancestor of each project's primitives/features). 'lint'/'dsDrift' = the commands that run eslint+check-motion and the drift report. The two gates (check-motion.mjs, check-ds-drift.mjs) are scaffolded into the app's scripts/ from <skill>/scaffold/.

wabi-sabi-foundation.note: No standalone foundation export yet — the foundation is bundled in the pr-controller-prototypes export, so its baseline IS that export's _ds/. Don't copy it into a separate dir (duplicates + drifts on the next product sync). A standalone foundation import would give it its own design/.upstream/wabi-sabi-foundation/ baseline.

localPatches.$comment: Intentional, deliberate divergences of the app's .d.ts prop contracts from the upstream baseline. scripts/check-ds-drift.mjs suppresses these so only GENUINE missed-upstream changes surface (the signal/noise fix for the 3-way merge). Keys per component: added (app-only prop), retyped (prop type widened/narrowed on purpose), dropped (upstream prop intentionally omitted).

Toggle.note: local a11y: accessible name for a label-less switch (applied only when `label` is absent).

OrganicLoader.note: intentionally widened to `string` (any token name) — broader than upstream's OrganicLoaderTone union.

decomposition.$comment: Which app feature files each prototype (.dc.html) fans out into, so a re-sync edits the existing split instead of re-discovering it (and risking regressing local edits). Verify on a structural change; dc-extract.mjs helps re-derive props/tables.

referenceOnly.$comment: Prototype logic that is NOT ported — the daemon owns it. Translate behaviors, not this scaffolding.
referenceOnly.PR Controller.dc.html: makeController / buildItems / TAG_LANE / prInLane in-template routing is server-authoritative in the app (placements.mjs). State is the app-native useDashboard.js + cardProps.js + adapt.js — NOT a port of the prototype controller god-object.

pr-controller-prototypes.note: 2026-06-28 pull (changelog 3→20): (1) DS — OrganicLoader gained 11 'working blob' variants (seeker/diviner/swarm/vigil/wisp/sentinel/drowse/scan/saccade/comet/veil) + a per-instance `phase` self-stagger (useLayoutEffect that offsets each animated element so stacked loaders desync) + 19 new ws-blob-/ws-swarm-/ws-gaze-/ws-vigil-/ws-blink-/ws-droop/ws-scan/ws-saccade/ws-sweep-wide keyframes in tokens/base.css. The blob variants keep their `animation` shorthand INLINE (not a composed .ws-loader-* class) because the self-stagger reads inline styles — the existing 8 ambient glyphs are unchanged (composed classes + currentColor). (2) AgentWorking (the agent-working row AND branch-conflict rebase, which delegates to it) switched its loader ripple@22 → scan@40. (3) ThreadRow gained the multi-approach variant: a thread carrying `approaches[]` (1–3 {title,body,trade?,reply?}) renders selectable sage radio-cards with trade-off tags; the picked one drives the drafted reply and is what onApprove(index) stages (local choice state). Plumbed `Approach`/`approaches?` through types.ts ThreadWithDisposition + wire.ts WireThread + adapt.ts passthrough — but the worker does NOT emit multiple approaches yet, so it's DORMANT in prod (daemon only sets single suggestedApproach); remaining backend work is tracked in TODO.md. (4) New 'Prompt tracer' Settings tab (PromptTracer.jsx) — a read-only explainer of the net worker prompt with a scenario picker + sensitivity ladder + source-colour-coded segments; placed between 'Worker sensitivity' and 'Appearance'. (5) Settings-modal backdrop blur 2px→8px (the card-detail modal stays 2px). BASELINE REFRESHED to a verbatim mirror of the export, EXCLUDING binary noise (screenshots/, uploads/, .thumbnail) + the gitignored runtime (support.js, _ds_bundle.js); Net Prompt.dc.html→Prompt Tracer.dc.html rename handled by the wholesale rsync. Verified: typecheck (daemon+react), lint (eslint+check-motion 0 violations), ds-drift (clean), build, 209/209 daemon tests, + live visual check of all four surfaces. NOTE: the prior baseline's incidental Header.dc.html (· → \u00b7) + CLAUDE.md (added Claude-Design _changelog.json instructions) diffs are export regeneration, not design changes.
